name: Cloudflare
focus: Edge security, CDN, DNS, and website acceleration
description: Cloudflare operates an edge network: many small, distributed server locations placed close to internet users. Instead of sending every website request back to one faraway server, Cloudflare can answer DNS, block attacks, cache files, and run security checks near the visitor.
metro: San Jose / Silicon Valley, United States
infrastructure: Anycast edge POPs, cache servers, DNS resolvers, WAF/security filtering, bot protection, and peering with local ISPs.
trafficRole: Answers DNS, blocks attacks, and serves cached pages before traffic has to travel back to an origin server.
reliabilityImpact: If one edge location is busy or unavailable, anycast routing can shift users to another nearby Cloudflare site.
userImpact: A student opening a website may get images, scripts, and security checks from a nearby Cloudflare edge instead of a distant origin server.

name: Google Cloud
focus: Hyperscale cloud platform and private global backbone
description: Google Cloud provides cloud regions, storage, databases, AI systems, edge caches, and private backbone routing. The important idea is that many app requests can enter Google's network near the user, then move across Google's own high-capacity fiber instead of crossing the public internet for the whole trip.
metro: Singapore, near the Jurong West data-center cluster
infrastructure: Cloud region capacity, private backbone routers, edge caches, load balancers, submarine-cable connectivity, and metro-fiber links.
trafficRole: Hosts apps and moves user requests onto Google private fiber instead of leaving every packet on the public internet.
reliabilityImpact: Regional capacity and backbone routing keep Southeast Asia traffic close, with alternate paths when a link or facility is degraded.
userImpact: When an app runs in a nearby Google Cloud region, users usually wait less because the request does not need to travel across an ocean first.
mapQuery: Google Data Center Jurong West Singapore

name: Microsoft Azure
focus: Enterprise cloud platform, Microsoft services, and private WAN
description: Microsoft Azure is a global cloud platform used to run applications, databases, identity systems, virtual machines, and enterprise services. Azure regions are connected by Microsoft's private wide-area network, so business traffic can stay on managed backbone paths for much of its journey.
infrastructure: Azure region data centers, availability zones, compute clusters, storage systems, identity services, and Microsoft WAN connectivity.
trafficRole: Keeps Microsoft 365, enterprise apps, cloud workloads, and private connections near European users and business networks.
reliabilityImpact: Availability zones and private WAN routing reduce dependency on one building, one carrier, or one public internet path.
userImpact: A company using Microsoft 365 or an Azure-hosted app can serve European users from nearby regional infrastructure.

name: AWS
focus: Large cloud region, availability zones, and direct connectivity
description: AWS runs cloud regions made of multiple availability zones. Each zone contains separated data-center facilities with power, cooling, networking, servers, and storage. Many everyday apps use AWS for websites, APIs, databases, video processing, backups, and internal business systems.
infrastructure: Large cloud region, availability zones, dense fiber, Direct Connect facilities, load balancers, storage systems, and nearby carrier-neutral data centers.
trafficRole: Hosts major web apps, APIs, databases, streaming backends, and private enterprise connections.
reliabilityImpact: Dense interconnection and multiple availability zones make this metro a common primary hosting and failover location.
userImpact: When a mobile app talks to its backend, the server or database may be running inside an AWS region like Northern Virginia.
mapQuery: Amazon Web Services Data Center Ashburn Virginia

name: Meta
focus: Social platform backbone, media storage, and global delivery
description: Meta operates large data centers and a private network to move photos, videos, messages, and feed updates between users and data centers. The goal is to keep high-volume social traffic on controlled, optimized paths instead of depending only on public transit providers.
infrastructure: Private backbone routers, large compute and storage campuses, media caches, machine-learning capacity, and long-haul fiber interconnects.
trafficRole: Moves photos, reels, messages, feed updates, and app telemetry between users, regional caches, and Meta data centers.
reliabilityImpact: Private backbone control lets Meta steer traffic around congestion faster than relying only on third-party transit.
userImpact: A video uploaded in one region can be stored, processed, copied, and delivered from infrastructure closer to other viewers.

name: Akamai
focus: Global content delivery, security, and edge traffic management
description: Akamai is one of the oldest and largest content delivery networks. It places servers inside or near many access networks, so files such as images, software downloads, and video chunks can be served close to users instead of from a distant origin.
infrastructure: Distributed CDN servers, media caches, DNS mapping systems, origin shielding, and security edge capacity inside provider networks.
trafficRole: Serves software downloads, images, video chunks, and protected web traffic from caches close to access networks.
reliabilityImpact: A broad cache footprint absorbs demand spikes and reduces load on distant origin data centers.
userImpact: When many people download the same update or stream the same popular content, Akamai can serve local cached copies.

name: Fastly
focus: Programmable edge cloud, CDN, and real-time delivery
description: Fastly runs an edge cloud used for CDN caching, security, API acceleration, and small pieces of logic that execute near users. This helps dynamic websites and APIs respond faster because some decisions happen at the edge instead of always returning to the origin server.
metro: San Francisco Bay Area, United States
infrastructure: Edge compute POPs, CDN cache nodes, TLS termination, API acceleration, web security controls, and real-time log delivery.
trafficRole: Runs website logic and caching at the edge so dynamic pages and APIs respond with less round-trip delay.
reliabilityImpact: Edge routing can drain traffic from a degraded POP and keep origin systems protected from sudden bursts.
userImpact: A news site or API can use Fastly to return frequently requested content quickly while still applying rules at the edge.

name: Oracle Cloud
focus: Cloud infrastructure for databases and enterprise workloads
description: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure is often used for enterprise systems, databases, analytics, virtual machines, and applications that need predictable performance. It gives organizations cloud regions where they can run business workloads close to users or close to other systems.
infrastructure: OCI cloud region, database infrastructure, compute clusters, storage systems, load balancing, and private connectivity for enterprises.
trafficRole: Runs data-heavy enterprise systems, managed databases, analytics, and backend services for business applications.
reliabilityImpact: Regional cloud capacity gives nearby workloads lower latency and another disaster-recovery target in the western US.
userImpact: A business application can keep its database and application servers in the same cloud region to reduce internal delay.
mapQuery: Oracle Cloud Data Center Phoenix Arizona

name: Alibaba Cloud
focus: Cloud platform for ecommerce, payments, and Asia regional workloads
description: Alibaba Cloud provides cloud compute, databases, storage, CDN, security, and data processing. It is especially important for high-volume ecommerce and payment traffic where response time and capacity are critical during demand spikes.
infrastructure: Cloud compute, object storage, payment and ecommerce infrastructure, regional backbone links, CDN capacity, and security services.
trafficRole: Supports ecommerce, payments, media, and enterprise workloads that need high capacity across China and Asia.
reliabilityImpact: Local cloud and cache placement keeps high-volume shopping and payment traffic from crossing unnecessary long routes.
userImpact: During a large online sale, regional cloud capacity helps process many product, payment, and inventory requests quickly.

name: Tencent Cloud
focus: Cloud platform for games, chat, video, and media delivery
description: Tencent Cloud supports applications that are sensitive to latency, including games, chat, livestreaming, and media platforms. For these services, stable routing matters because users notice lag, jitter, or slow media startup almost immediately.
infrastructure: Cloud region services, game backends, media delivery nodes, cross-border connectivity, CDN POPs, and peering.
trafficRole: Carries game sessions, chat, livestream, and app traffic where stable latency matters more than raw bandwidth alone.
reliabilityImpact: A Hong Kong hub gives regional services more route choices between mainland China, Southeast Asia, and global networks.
userImpact: A multiplayer game can place backend services closer to players so commands and updates arrive with less delay.

name: Equinix
note: Data center and IX interconnect hub
description: Equinix operates carrier-neutral data centers. That means many networks, clouds, CDNs, and enterprises can place equipment in the same facility and connect directly with short cross-connect cables instead of sending traffic through longer outside paths.
infrastructure: Carrier-neutral colocation, meet-me rooms, cross-connects, cloud on-ramps, internet exchange access, and network interconnection.
trafficRole: Lets ISPs, clouds, CDNs, and enterprises exchange traffic directly instead of sending it through longer transit paths.
reliabilityImpact: Many providers in one campus create backup paths and lower latency for European interconnection.
userImpact: If two networks connect inside the same data center, traffic can move across a short local link instead of traveling through extra networks.

name: Digital Realty
focus: Large colocation platform and enterprise interconnection
description: Digital Realty provides colocation and interconnection facilities where companies can place their own servers, network equipment, and private links near cloud and carrier partners. These facilities are part of the physical layer behind many cloud and enterprise services.
infrastructure: Colocation suites, meet-me rooms, cross-connects, cloud connectivity, carrier access, power, cooling, and enterprise infrastructure space.
trafficRole: Gives companies a neutral place to place servers beside carriers, clouds, and private network partners.
reliabilityImpact: Central US location and provider diversity make it useful for regional failover and low-latency enterprise routing.
userImpact: A company can connect its private servers directly to cloud providers and carriers from the same data-center campus.

name: NTT Communications
note: Tier-1 backbone and data center hub
focus: Global IP backbone, enterprise network, and data centers
description: NTT Communications operates global backbone connectivity, enterprise network services, and data centers. A Tier-1 backbone can reach the rest of the internet through settlement-free peering, which means fewer handoffs and more direct routing options.
infrastructure: Tier-1 IP backbone, enterprise network services, data centers, subsea cable access, exchange connectivity, and cloud connectivity.
trafficRole: Carries international transit, enterprise WAN traffic, and Asia-Pacific routes between clouds and access networks.
reliabilityImpact: Tier-1 reach gives direct global paths and fewer handoffs for traffic leaving or entering Japan.
userImpact: Traffic from Japan to another region can use backbone paths with fewer intermediate networks, which can improve consistency.

name: Lumen
focus: Long-haul fiber, IP transit, and enterprise networking
description: Lumen operates long-haul and metro fiber networks used to move traffic between cities, data centers, cloud regions, and enterprise sites. This type of provider is important because clouds and data centers still need physical fiber paths between locations.
infrastructure: Long-haul fiber routes, IP transit, wavelength services, metro fiber, Ethernet, and enterprise network connectivity.
trafficRole: Moves bulk traffic between cities, cloud regions, data centers, and business sites across backbone corridors.
reliabilityImpact: Multiple long-haul paths help route traffic around regional fiber cuts or congestion.
userImpact: A video stream or cloud backup may cross a provider backbone like Lumen between metro areas before reaching its destination.

name: Deutsche Telekom
focus: European telecom backbone, access network, and peering
description: Deutsche Telekom connects residential, mobile, enterprise, and international traffic across Europe. Telecom operators are important because they own access networks close to users and backbone networks that move traffic toward clouds, exchanges, and content providers.
infrastructure: European carrier backbone, mobile and fixed access networks, transit, peering, enterprise connectivity, and interconnection near Frankfurt.
trafficRole: Connects residential, mobile, enterprise, and cloud traffic through one of Europe's densest interconnect metros.
reliabilityImpact: Frankfurt peering density gives European traffic many short alternate paths during failures or congestion.
userImpact: A home or mobile user in Europe may reach cloud and content networks through a telecom backbone before traffic enters a data center.

name: Arelion
focus: Tier-1 internet backbone and global transit
description: Arelion is a global internet backbone provider. Backbone providers connect networks to each other at large scale, carrying traffic between countries, continents, cloud platforms, data centers, and access networks.
infrastructure: Tier-1 IP backbone, global transit, peering, wavelength services, cloud connectivity, and Nordic fiber routes.
trafficRole: Carries traffic between networks that need global reach without building their own worldwide backbone.
reliabilityImpact: Tier-1 connectivity adds diverse international paths for Nordic and European traffic.
userImpact: When one network needs to reach another network far away, a backbone provider can carry traffic across major internet corridors.

name: Zayo
focus: Fiber infrastructure for data centers, carriers, and enterprises
description: Zayo provides fiber infrastructure: dark fiber, wavelengths, data-center interconnects, and high-capacity routes. Fiber providers are the physical road system that connects cloud regions, mobile towers, carrier hotels, and company networks.
infrastructure: Metro fiber, dark fiber, wavelengths, data-center interconnects, mobile backhaul, and long-haul routes.
trafficRole: Links data centers, mobile towers, cloud on-ramps, and enterprise sites with high-capacity private paths.
reliabilityImpact: Fiber diversity across metro and long-haul corridors helps reroute around local construction damage or carrier outages.
userImpact: A cloud provider, mobile carrier, or enterprise may lease Zayo fiber to connect facilities without using the public internet.

name: Starlink
focus: Low-earth-orbit satellite access and ground network handoff
description: Starlink connects users through low-earth-orbit satellites. A user terminal sends traffic to satellites overhead, and the network eventually hands that traffic to ground gateways connected to terrestrial fiber and internet backbones.
infrastructure: Satellite network operations, ground gateway planning, user-terminal ecosystem, orbital links, and terrestrial fiber handoff.
trafficRole: Relays remote user traffic through low-earth-orbit satellites, then hands it off to ground networks and internet backbones.
reliabilityImpact: Satellite paths provide coverage and backup where fiber, mobile, or microwave links are unavailable or damaged.
userImpact: A remote home, ship, aircraft, or emergency site can reach the internet even where normal wired networks are limited.
